highlights.summary: Introduce NightscoutUploadSerializer to serialize, coalesce, and run Nightscout upload runs per NightscoutUploadPipeline (ensures at most one in-flight run per pipeline and one queued follow-up). BaseNightscoutManager upload entry points now call into the serializer; internal upload implementations were renamed to performUpload*() and are executed in the serializer-run context. Removed the prior per-pipeline 2s throttle/Combine machinery. Unit tests for serializer concurrency, queuing, coalescing, reentrancy, and pipeline independence were added.
highlights.userFacingImpact: Fixes duplicate Nightscout treatment uploads caused by overlapping upload runs by ensuring no two runs of the same pipeline overlap. Uploads now start immediately (removed fixed 2s throttle) and queued follow-up requests are coalesced rather than silently dropped. App Intent/awaitable upload semantics are preserved but routed through the serializer. Tests covering the new behavior were added; run the test suite during review.

sources.title: gordolio/Trio#1290: Fix duplicate Nightscout treatment uploads by serializing per-pipeline runs
nightscout/Trio#1290: Fix duplicate Nightscout treatment uploads by serializing per-pipeline runs
Fix duplicate Nightscout treatment uploads by serializing per-pipeline runs

highlights.summary: Remove the Javascript oref implementation and related JS resources/build references; convert algorithm code to use native Swift types instead of JSON-encoded intermediates (beginning removal of JSON marshalling).
highlights.userFacingImpact: The Javascript oref implementation and its JS bundles/resources were removed; the app now relies on the Swift oref implementation. Algorithm inputs/outputs are being refactored to use native Swift types (e.g., makeProfile and glucose conversion). If you relied on the removed Javascript worker, script files, or the removed useJavascriptOref setting, those runtime/code-path references are no longer present.

sources.title: gordolio/Trio#1317: Remove the Javascript algorithm and JSON marshaling from Trio
nightscout/Trio#1317: Remove the Javascript algorithm and JSON marshaling from Trio
[Part 3 of 6] Remove JSON encoding for glucose
Refactor to move logic with GlucoseStorage

highlights.summary: Remove the OpenAPSSwift bridging layer and refactor OpenAPS to call algorithm generators with native typed arguments instead of JSON strings. Deleted OpenAPSSwift.swift and OrefFunctionResult.swift; simplified JSONBridge decoders and replaced multiple generator functions with direct generator types (IobGenerator, MealGenerator, AutosensGenerator, DeterminationGenerator). Simplified Determination model code.
highlights.userFacingImpact: DeterminationError messages are now exposed raw instead of being converted into a generic APSError; determination-related error messages may become directly visible where previously a generic message was shown. Passing native typed inputs into generators may affect dosing logic; a human review is required to confirm no unintended dosing or safety implications.

items.summary: Fix delete confirmation dialogs for Override and Temp Target presets so they no longer auto-dismiss. Replaced two-state-variable dialog driving inside ForEach with single optional delete targets and a confirmationDialog attached once to the view. Adjusted action button helpers.
items.userFacingImpact: Fixes unreliable deletion when swiping Override or Temp Target presets: the confirmation dialog no longer auto-dismisses immediately, allowing users to confirm deletions successfully.

Fix Temp Target and Override delete confirmation auto-dismissing

items.summary: Remove explicit allowsConstrainedNetworkAccess = false from all Nightscout upload URLRequest constructions, restoring default URLRequest behavior.
items.userFacingImpact: iOS will again decide how to handle constrained network access per system settings rather than the app forcing disallow; this addresses observed "Constrained path prohibited" upload errors. The patch removes the explicit flag but does not include test telemetry proving improved reliability, so human review and testing are recommended.

items.summary: Replace CoreData -> JSON string -> [CarbsEntry] pipeline with direct CoreData -> [CarbsEntry] mapping. Added CarbsStorage.getCarbsForAlgorithm(additionalCarbs:carbsDate:) and mapping helpers; removed the CarbEntryStored JSON encoder. Added tests (CarbsNativeConversionTests) validating parity with prior JSON round-trip except for documented id/date differences.
items.userFacingImpact: Intended to preserve algorithm inputs via native mapping; golden tests assert field-for-field equivalence for covered cases except ids and millisecond date precision. Because this changes how carb entries are constructed and delivered to the dosing algorithm, potential behavioral differences are possible; a human review and regression test in staging is recommended before release.

items.summary: Replace CoreData -> DTOs -> JSON string -> [PumpHistoryEvent] pipeline with direct CoreData -> [PumpHistoryEvent] mapping. Added PumpEventStored mapping helpers and adjusted OpenAPS.parsePumpHistory and call sites to accept native [PumpHistoryEvent]. Removed JSON-based pumpHistory parsing helper and added tests. Temp-basal subtype handling now nullifies temp when tempType is unrecognized or nil (instead of failing decode).
items.userFacingImpact: Intended to be functionally identical for recorded pump events while removing an intermediate JSON round-trip. The deliberate change to temp basal subtype handling makes parsing more robust (unrecognized/nil tempType becomes nullified rather than causing decode failure). Because this modifies algorithm inputs, a human reviewer should confirm equivalence for dosing safety.

includedPullRequests.title: Fix duplicate Nightscout treatment uploads by serializing per-pipeline runs
includedPullRequests.body:
## Problem

Trio occasionally uploads the same treatment to Nightscout twice — most visibly Exercise entries for overrides enacted via Shortcuts or remote commands. Reproduced with an APNS remote command producing two documents with millisecond-identical `created_at` and consecutive Mongo `_id`s, i.e. two near-simultaneous POSTs:

- Enacting an override via App Intents / remote control triggers uploads twice: the Core Data save fires the upload pipeline, and `.willUpdateOverrideConfiguration` fires a direct, unthrottled upload. Both fetch the same rows still marked `isUploadedToNS == false` before either has marked them uploaded.
- Nightscout's treatment upsert is a non-atomic find-then-insert (no unique index), so concurrent identical POSTs both insert. Sequential re-posts are deduped server-side; only concurrent ones slip through. (API v3 has the same race, so switching API versions wouldn't help.)
- The 2s throttle didn't prevent this: it only spaced out task starts within one trigger path, never waited for the previous upload to finish, and with `latest: false` silently dropped triggers arriving inside its window. Carb edits had a third, entirely unthrottled path (`HistoryStateModel` calls `uploadCarbs()` directly).

## Solution

Replace the per-pipeline subject/throttle machinery with `NightscoutUploadSerializer`, an actor that coalesces and serializes upload runs per pipeline:

- An idle pipeline starts a run immediately; while a run is in flight, exactly one follow-up is queued and further requests coalesce into it.
- No two runs of a pipeline ever overlap: a follow-up re-fetches only after the previous run marked its rows uploaded, so it finds nothing left to send. Queued runs double as retry attempts after network failures.
- The upload routine is provided once at init; `request(_:)`/`run(_:)` take only the pipeline, so every entry point (Core Data triggers, App Intents, remote control, direct calls) is covered with no call-site changes.
- App Intents semantics are preserved: `uploadOverrides()`/`uploadTempTargets()` are awaitable, so `.didUpdateOverrideConfiguration` still posts only after the upload attempt finished.

## Benefits

- No more duplicate treatments, regardless of how many trigger paths fire for the same change.
- Uploads start immediately — the fixed 2-second throttle delay is gone.
- No dropped triggers (the old throttle discarded a second change arriving within 2 s; it stayed unuploaded until the next unrelated trigger).
- `uploadDeviceStatus()` no longer throws; its only caller was the deleted pipeline dispatch, and errors are logged in `performUpload(for:)`.

Unit tests cover the serializer: no overlap under concurrency, queued-run ordering, burst coalescing into a single follow-up, completion semantics, and pipeline independence.

includedPullRequests.title: Remove the Javascript algorithm and JSON marshaling from Trio
includedPullRequests.body:
This PR removes the Javascript implementation of the oref algorithm, leaving only the Swift version. It also cleans up some prior artifacts from the previous algorithm implemented in Javascript.

Originally this was split up into 6 different PRs and code reviewed before being merged into a feature branch. This PR is simply merging this feature into dev.
* https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1284
* https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1287
* https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1291
* https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1294
* https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1311
* https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1316

I tested it both in the simulator and in vivo.

includedPullRequests.title: [Part 4 of 6] Remove JSON encoding for carbs
includedPullRequests.body:
This PR replaces the old CoreData -> JSON string -> [CarbsEntry]
pipeline with one that goes directly from CoreData to
[CarbsEntry]. The conversion is almost identical with two changes
coming from (1) including IDs (the old way didn't copy IDs over to the
CarbsEntry objects) and (2) using full precision dates instead of
encoded / decoded ISO string dates.

includedPullRequests.title: [Part 4 of 6] Remove JSON encoding for carbs
includedPullRequests.body:
This PR replaces the old CoreData -> JSON string -> [CarbsEntry]
pipeline with one that goes directly from CoreData to
[CarbsEntry]. The conversion is almost identical with two changes
coming from (1) including IDs (the old way didn't copy IDs over to the
CarbsEntry objects) and (2) using full precision dates instead of
encoded / decoded ISO string dates.

includedPullRequests.title: [Part 5 of 6] Remove JSON encoding for pump events
includedPullRequests.body:
This commit removes the old:
* CoreData -> DTOs -> JSON string -> [PumpHistoryEvent]

pipeline and replaces it with a direct pipeline:
* CoreData -> [PumpHistoryEvent]

The result of the two pipelines should be identical, except for the
handling of the subtype tempType of temp basal events, which we
nullify if we get an unrecognized string vs crashing as the old
pipeline would. This change is safe as Trio only ever records
"absolute" types and the value is copied but never actually used by
the algorithm.

includedPullRequests.title: [Part 5 of 6] Remove JSON encoding for pump events

nullify if we get an unrecognized string vs crashing as the old
pipeline would. This change is safe as Trio only ever records
"absolute" types and the value is copied but never actually used by
the algorithm.

includedPullRequests.title: Fix Temp Target and Override delete confirmation auto-dismissing
includedPullRequests.body:
Fixes #1314

Deleting a Temp Target or Override preset by swiping was unreliable. The delete confirmation only appeared for a moment before auto-dismissing, so the preset often couldn't be deleted.

The confirmation dialog was attached to the ForEach inside the List and driven by two state variables set at the same time, which made it dismiss itself. Reworked it to match the History screen: a single optional drives the dialog via presenting:, attached once to the view. Applied to both Temp Targets and Overrides.

includedPullRequests.title: Fix Temp Target and Override delete confirmation auto-dismissing
includedPullRequests.body:
Fixes #1314

Deleting a Temp Target or Override preset by swiping was unreliable. The delete confirmation only appeared for a moment before auto-dismissing, so the preset often couldn't be deleted.

The confirmation dialog was attached to the ForEach inside the List and driven by two state variables set at the same time, which made it dismiss itself. Reworked it to match the History screen: a single optional drives the dialog via presenting:, attached once to the view. Applied to both Temp Targets and Overrides.

includedPullRequests.title: [Part 6 of 6] Remove OpenAPSSwift.swift and use native arguments
includedPullRequests.body:
This commit is the last part of our removal of the JSON conversion we
used to do when we ran the oref algorithm using Javascript. In this
commit, we remove the OpenAPSSwift file and just call the underlying
algorithm functions directly from OpenAPS.

One change in functionality is that we expose raw DeterminationError
messages instead of catching them and converting them to APSError
exceptions with a generic "No determination error" error
message. These exception messages will be user visible when they
happen.

One change in functionality is that we expose raw DeterminationError
messages instead of catching them and converting them to APSError
exceptions with a generic "No determination error" error
message. These exception messages will be user visible when they
happen.

includedPullRequests.title: Remove explicit allowsConstrainedNetworkAccess setting for Nightscout uploads
includedPullRequests.body:
## Summary

This PR removes the explicit `allowsConstrainedNetworkAccess` setting from all Nightscout upload requests.

This restores the default `URLRequest` behavior and lets iOS decide how constrained network access should be handled according to the user's system settings.

## Background

While investigating intermittent Nightscout upload failures, repeated `Constrained path prohibited` errors were observed in the logs. This PR follows the review discussion and removes the explicit flag instead of overriding the system default.
